title: Acyclicity

tex_statement.html: A CDMG $G=(J,V,E,L)$ is called acyclic if there does not exist any non-trivial directed walk from $v$ to itself in $G$ for any node $v \in G$.

lean_code_with_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ∀ v ∈ G, ¬ ∃ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ 0 < p.length
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsAcyclic (G : CDMG Node) : Prop :=
  ∀ v ∈ G, ¬ ∃ p : Walk G v v, p.IsDirectedWalk ∧ 0 < p.length
addition_to_the_LN: [nontrivial_directed_walk_not_defined_in_block] A non-trivial directed walk is a directed walk $v_0 \tuh \cdots \tuh v_n$ with $n \ge 1$ (i.e., one that traverses at least one edge); the trivial walk consisting of a single node $v_0$ is excluded. In particular, a self-loop $v \tuh v$ constitutes a non-trivial directed walk from $v$ to itself, so an acyclic CDMG contains no self-loops on any $v \in V$.
